<div class="_PageLayoutConstraints_1vcwt_1"><div class="_PageLayoutConstraintsInner_1vcwt_14"><hr class="_SectionDivider_19vap_15"/></div></div>
<div class="_PageLayoutConstraints_1vcwt_1"><div class="_PageLayoutConstraintsInner_1vcwt_14"><section class="_OneColumnSection_1s5yd_1"><h2><div id="api-availability" class="_Wrapper_gm9zu_1 group/hashtarget"><a data-markdown="remove" class="_LinkHashTarget_gm9zu_14" href="/docs/api/functions/latest#api-availability" data-discover="true"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#LinkMedium"></use></svg><span class="visuallyHidden">Anchor to API availability</span></a>API availability</div></h2><p><strong>All plans</strong>: Except as noted in individual API pages, stores on any plan can use public apps that are distributed through the Shopify App Store and contain functions.</p><p><strong>Shopify Plus</strong>: Only stores on a <a href="https://help.shopify.com/manual/intro-to-shopify/pricing-plans/plans-features/shopify-plus-plan" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="external-link"><span class="external-link-label">Shopify Plus </span><span class="external-link-tail"><span class="external-link-tail-word">plan</span><span class="external-link-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span></span></a> can use <a href="https://help.shopify.com/manual/apps/app-types/custom-apps" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="external-link"><span class="external-link-label">custom </span><span class="external-link-tail"><span class="external-link-tail-word">apps</span><span class="external-link-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span></span></a> that contain Shopify Function APIs.</p></section></div></div>
<div class="_PageLayoutConstraints_1vcwt_1"><div class="_PageLayoutConstraintsInner_1vcwt_14"><hr class="_SectionDivider_19vap_15"/></div></div>
<div class="_PageLayoutConstraints_1vcwt_1"><div class="_PageLayoutConstraintsInner_1vcwt_14"><section class="_OneColumnSection_1s5yd_1"><h2><div id="limitations" class="_Wrapper_gm9zu_1 group/hashtarget"><a data-markdown="remove" class="_LinkHashTarget_gm9zu_14" href="/docs/api/functions/latest#limitations" data-discover="true"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#LinkMedium"></use></svg><span class="visuallyHidden">Anchor to Limitations</span></a>Limitations</div></h2><p>Your function must adhere to our resource limits. Performance is critical because functions run in the context of key purchase flows like discounts and checkout. Slow or resource-intensive functions can negatively impact the customer experience and potentially prevent customers from completing their purchases.</p><p>We strongly recommend that you use <a href="/docs/apps/build/functions/programming-languages/rust-for-functions">Rust</a> as the most performant language choice. Rust&#x27;s memory safety and zero-cost abstractions help ensure your function runs efficiently and stays within resource limits, even when processing large carts or complex business logic.</p><p>The following apply to all functions:</p><ul>
<li>Apps can reference only their own functions in GraphQL Admin API mutations, such as <a href="/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/discountAutomaticAppCreate"><code>discountAutomaticAppCreate</code></a> and <a href="/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/cartTransformCreate"><code>cartTransformCreate</code></a>. Referencing a function from another app results in a <code>Function not found</code> error.</li>
<li>Shopify doesn&#x27;t allow nondeterminism in functions, which means that you can&#x27;t use any randomizing or clock functionality in your functions.</li>
<li>You can&#x27;t debug your function by printing out <code>STDOUT</code> or <code>STDERR</code>.</li>
<li>The Shopify App Store doesn&#x27;t permit apps that provide dynamic editing and execution of function code.</li>
<li>Some functions support network access, refer to <a href="/docs/apps/build/functions/network-access">network access</a> for more information. You can also pre-populate data by using metafields on products and customers, or passing data using cart attributes.</li>
</ul><h3><div id="fixed-limits" class="_Wrapper_gm9zu_1 group/hashtarget"><a data-markdown="remove" class="_LinkHashTarget_gm9zu_14" href="/docs/api/functions/latest#fixed-limits" data-discover="true"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#LinkMedium"></use></svg><span class="visuallyHidden">Anchor to Fixed limits</span></a>Fixed limits</div></h3><p>The following resource limits apply to all functions:</p><table><thead><tr><th>Resource</th><th>Limit</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Compiled binary size</td><td>256 kB</td></tr><tr><td>Runtime linear memory</td><td>10,000 kB</td></tr><tr><td>Runtime stack memory</td><td>512 kB</td></tr><tr><td>Logs written</td><td>1 kB (truncated)</td></tr></tbody></table><div class="MinorTheme-Note MinorTheme-Docs _Framed_i9sw8_48 _Notice_i9sw8_1 _NoticeMargin_15ynr_1" data-testid="notice-Note" data-markdown="remove"><div class="_Heading_i9sw8_71"><div class="_Icon_i9sw8_52"><span class="DefaultIcon"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#NoteMedium"></use></svg></span></div><div class="markdown _MarkdownRenderer_1wwby_4 _Label_i9sw8_56">Note</div></div><div class="_Body_i9sw8_66"><p>Function resource limits treat 1 kilobyte (kB) as 1000 bytes.</p></div></div><div hidden=""><strong>Note:</strong> <p>Function resource limits treat 1 kilobyte (kB) as 1000 bytes.</p></div><h3><div id="dynamic-limits" class="_Wrapper_gm9zu_1 group/hashtarget"><a data-markdown="remove" class="_LinkHashTarget_gm9zu_14" href="/docs/api/functions/latest#dynamic-limits" data-discover="true"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#LinkMedium"></use></svg><span class="visuallyHidden">Anchor to Dynamic limits</span></a>Dynamic limits</div></h3><p>Certain limits are dynamic and scale based on the number of line items in a cart.
For carts with more than 200 line items, these values will scale proportionally as the number of cart lines increases. Calculated limits for a function execution are available in your <a href="https://dev.shopify.com/dashboard/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="external-link"><span class="external-link-label">Dev </span><span class="external-link-tail"><span class="external-link-tail-word">Dashboard</span><span class="external-link-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span></span></a> and can be <a href="/docs/apps/build/functions/test-debug-functions#execute-the-function-locally-using-shopify-cli">tested with Shopify CLI</a>.</p><p>The following resource limits apply to all functions for carts with up to 200 line items:</p><table><thead><tr><th>Resource</th><th>Limit (up to 200 line items)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Execution <a href="https://webassembly.github.io/spec/core/syntax/instructions.html" class="external-link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer"><span class="external-link-label">instruction </span><span class="external-link-tail"><span class="external-link-tail-word">count</span><span class="external-link-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span></span></a></td><td>11 million instructions</td></tr><tr><td>Function input</td><td>128 kB</td></tr><tr><td>Function output</td><td>20 kB<br/><br/>This limit doesn&#x27;t support bulk price transformations across all line items. Use <a href="/docs/api/functions/latest/discount">discount functions</a>, <a href="/docs/apps/build/b2b/manage-catalogs">B2B catalogs</a>, or target specific products instead.</td></tr></tbody></table><div class="MinorTheme-Note MinorTheme-Docs _Framed_i9sw8_48 _Notice_i9sw8_1 _NoticeMargin_15ynr_1" data-testid="notice-Note" data-markdown="remove"><div class="_Heading_i9sw8_71"><div class="_Icon_i9sw8_52"><span class="DefaultIcon"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#NoteMedium"></use></svg></span></div><div class="markdown _MarkdownRenderer_1wwby_4 _Label_i9sw8_56">Note</div></div><div class="_Body_i9sw8_66"><p>Function resource limits treat 1 kilobyte (kB) as 1000 bytes.</p></div></div><div hidden=""><strong>Note:</strong> <p>Function resource limits treat 1 kilobyte (kB) as 1000 bytes.</p></div><h3><div id="input-query-limits" class="_Wrapper_gm9zu_1 group/hashtarget"><a data-markdown="remove" class="_LinkHashTarget_gm9zu_14" href="/docs/api/functions/latest#input-query-limits" data-discover="true"><svg width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#LinkMedium"></use></svg><span class="visuallyHidden">Anchor to Input query limits</span></a>Input query limits</div></h3><p>The following limitations apply to input queries:</p><ul>
<li>
<p>The maximum size for an input query, excluding comments, is 3000 bytes.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Metafields with values exceeding 10,000 bytes in size will not be returned.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Field arguments and input query variables of <a href="https://graphql.org/learn/schema/#lists-and-non-null" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="external-link"><span class="external-link-label"></span><span class="external-link-tail"><span class="external-link-tail-word">list</span><span class="external-link-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span></span></a> type can&#x27;t exceed 100 elements.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Function input queries can have a maximum calculated query cost of 30. The following table describes the input query field costs for functions:</p>
<table><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Example</th><th>Cost value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><code>__typename</code></td><td></td><td>0</td></tr><tr><td>Any field that returns a <code>Metafield</code> object</td><td><code>metafield</code> on a <code>Product</code> object</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Any field on a <code>Metafield</code> object</td><td><code>value</code></td><td>0</td></tr><tr><td><code>metaobject</code> root field</td><td><code>metaobject(handle: &quot;...&quot;)</code> or <code>metaobject(id: &quot;...&quot;)</code></td><td>1</td></tr><tr><td><code>field(key:)</code> on a <code>Metaobject</code></td><td><code>field(key: &quot;price-tier&quot;)</code></td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td><code>hasAnyTag</code></td><td></td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td><code>hasTags</code></td><td></td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Any field on a <code>HasTagResponse</code> object</td><td><code>hasTag</code></td><td>0</td></tr><tr><td><code>inAnyCollection</code></td><td></td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td><code>inCollections</code></td><td></td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Any field on a <code>CollectionMembership</code> object</td><td><code>isMember</code></td><td>0</td></tr><tr><td>Other leaf nodes</td><td><code>id</code> or <code>sku</code> on a <code>ProductVariant</code> object</td><td>1</td></tr></tbody></table>
</li>
</ul></section></div></div>
<div class="_PageLayoutConstraints_1vcwt_1"><div class="_PageLayoutConstraintsInner_1vcwt_14"><hr class="_SectionDivider_19vap_15"/></div></div>

{
    if (!window.history.state || !window.history.state.key) {
      let key = Math.random().toString(32).slice(2);
      window.history.replaceState({ key }, "");
    }
    try {
      let positions = JSON.parse(sessionStorage.getItem(storageKey2) || "{}");
      let storedY = positions[restoreKey || window.history.state.key];
      if (typeof storedY === "number") {
        window.scrollTo(0, storedY);
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(error);
      sessionStorage.removeItem(storageKey2);
    }
  })("react-router-scroll-positions", null)</script><script>((storageKey2, restoreKey) => {

Functions execute in a\\nspecific sequence during checkout, and each function depends on data from earlier steps. This sequencing is important\\nbecause:`}),(0,e.jsxs)(n.ul,{children:[`\\n`,(0,e.jsx)(n.li,{children:\\\"Your function's input data comes from previous checkout operations.\\\"}),`\\n`,(0,e.jsx)(n.li,{children:\\\"The logic of your function might change, depending on where it's executed during the checkout process.\\\"}),`\\n`,(0,e.jsx)(n.li,{children:\\\"Understanding this flow helps you build more reliable and efficient functions.\\\"}),`\\n`]}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\\\"For example, when a customer adds items to their cart and proceeds to checkout, several functions might run in sequence:\\\"}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:`First, functions that change the pricing and presentation of items in a cart run. Then, functions that calculate discounts execute.\\nFinally, functions that validate the cart contents run.`}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:`Each step builds on the data from previous steps, so that a cart validation function can't run until after\\ndiscount calculations are complete.`}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\\\"Use this diagram to help you understand when each function runs during checkout, and plan your function's implementation accordingly.\\\"})]}),(0,e.jsx)(i,{secondary:\\\"true\\\",children:(0,e.jsx)(p,{name:\\\"FunctionsSequencing\\\",darkName:\\\"FunctionsSequencingDark\\\",displayRightColumn:!0})})]})}),`\\n`,(0,e.jsx)(h,{}),`\\n`,(0,e.jsxs)(u,{children:[(0,e.jsxs)(l,{children:[(0,e.jsxs)(i,{primary:\\\"true\\\",children:[(0,e.jsx)(n.h2,{children:\\\"Function extension target types\\\"}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\\\"Identifiers that specify where you're injecting code into Shopify. You'll also need to \\\",(0,e.jsx)(n.a,{href:\\\"/docs/apps/build/functions/network-access\\\",children:\\\"request network access for Functions\\\"}),\\\", as it's not currently available on development stores or in a developer preview. However, there are exceptions for some Function APIs. For information on fetch target access for a specific API, refer to that Function's API reference page.\\\"]})}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\\\"A mechanism for retrieving the data from a third party provider and passing the data to the run target.\\\"}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\\\"Shopify calls the fetch target before the run target. Shopify makes the HTTP call on your behalf, which makes the fetch results available to the run target. This ensures that the run target has access to data from an external source.\\\"}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\\\"Returning a network request is optional if it's not necessary for a specific function execution.\\\"})]}),(0,e.jsxs)(c,{id:\\\"Run-target\\\",children:[(0,e.jsx)(d,{id:\\\"Run-target\\\",children:\\\"Run target\\\"}),(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:`\\nAn extension point that enables you to
